sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: APO Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Apollo Global Management, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm is encouraging investors of Apollo Global Management, Inc. (NYSE: APO) to join a class action lawsuit alleging securities fraud. The lawsuit claims Apollo made false and misleading statements regarding its relationship with Jeffrey Epstein, potentially harming its reputation. Investors who purchased securities between May 10, 2021, and February 21, 2026, suffered losses and have a deadline of May 1, 2026, to contact the firm.

feed.title: US regulator ends probe into Tesla's 'actually smart summon' feature after software fixes
feed.summary: The U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has closed its investigation into Tesla's "smart summon" feature after concluding that software updates addressed concerns. The feature, which allows remote movement of vehicles over short distances, was primarily linked to low-speed incidents causing minor property damage, with no reported injuries or fatalities. Regulators decided that the low frequency and severity of incidents, coupled with the implemented fixes, did not warrant further action.

feed.title: Master Boat Builders delivers Port Arthur LNG tug
feed.summary: Master Boat Builders has delivered "Jill," the sixth and final tugboat in the Rapport 2800 series, to Gulf LNG Tugs of Port Arthur. This tug, along with an identical sister vessel, is specifically designed to support Sempra Infrastructure's Port Arthur LNG export facility in Texas. The delivery completes a contract for four new tugboats for the facility, with Gulf LNG Tugs of Port Arthur being the largest provider of LNG terminal services in North America.

feed.title: Built for the Core: How AMD is Driving the 5G SA Era with High-Performant Data Center Infrastructure
feed.summary: AMD is playing a crucial role in enabling the 5G Standalone (SA) era by providing high-performant data center infrastructure. The company's EPYC™ processors and Instinct™ GPUs offer powerful compute density, low latency processing, and high scalability essential for 5G core networks and advanced capabilities like network slicing. AMD also focuses on energy efficiency and an open ecosystem to support telcos in building future-ready and sustainable 5G networks.

feed.title: Bunge Global SA stock: Strong growth amid agribusiness shifts – what investors need to know
feed.summary: Bunge Global SA is highlighted as a resilient agribusiness giant navigating complex global markets with strategic expansions and robust capital returns. The article emphasizes its role in essential sectors, its global diversification, and its proactive moves into high-demand areas like plant-based proteins, making it an attractive pick for investors seeking stability and long-term value. Analysts view Bunge as potentially undervalued, with strong fundamentals and strategic shifts aligning with industry tailwinds, despite inherent commodity price volatility risks.

feed.title: Q4 Earnings Roundup: GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) And The Rest Of The General Industrial Machinery Segment
feed.summary: GE Aerospace and other general industrial machinery companies experienced a satisfactory Q4, with revenues generally beating expectations, though share prices have declined. GE Aerospace exceeded revenue and adjusted operating income estimates, while Columbus McKinnon also showed strong performance against estimates despite stock downturns. Conversely, Otis missed revenue expectations, and Icahn Enterprises beat revenue but missed EPS estimates, while Kadant had strong revenue but weak full-year guidance.

feed.title: E-cigarette and Vape Market to Get an Explosive Growth |Juul
feed.summary: The global E-cigarette and Vape Market is projected to experience explosive growth, valued at USD 48.20 billion in 2026 and expected to reach USD 255.28 billion by 2033, with a CAGR of 26.9%. This growth is driven by rising industry demand, expanding applications, and technological advancements. The report by Coherent Market Insights provides a comprehensive analysis of market drivers, restraints, opportunities, and competitive landscapes, offering actionable insights for strategic decision-making.

feed.title: Chevron Resumes Leviathan Gas Production offshore Israel
feed.summary: Chevron has resumed normal production at the Leviathan gas and condensate field off the coast of Israel, weeks after a suspension due to the ongoing conflict with Iran. The production hiatus lasted 33 days, and operator NewMed Energy LP is considering seeking compensation from the State for the interruption. The field's capacity has been upgraded to approximately 14 billion cubic meters annually, with plans for further expansion to 21 billion cubic meters by 2029 through the Phase 1B project.

feed.title: Why We Downgraded This Big T. Rowe Price Fund
feed.summary: Morningstar downgraded the T. Rowe Price Equity Income Fund I Class (REIPX) to a Bronze rating from Silver due to its unremarkable stock-picking approach, specifically a lowered Process Pillar rating. The article also highlights upgrades for Lord Abbett Short Duration R6 (LDLVX) and American Funds Emerging Market Bond Class R6 (REGGX) to a Silver rating, praising their rigorous short-term bond approach and thoughtful multi-manager strategy, respectively. These changes reflect Morningstar's updated evaluations based on process, people, and parent pillars for these funds.

feed.title: Biotech layoff pick back up as 14 companies cut 745 jobs in Q1
feed.summary: In the first quarter of 2026, 14 biotech companies announced layoffs, resulting in 745 job cuts. This number marks an increase from the previous quarter, which saw 476 workers lose their jobs. Companies such as Tessera, Vertex, and Takeda were among those implementing cuts.

feed.title: The Ridiculously Nerdy Intel Bet That Could Rake in Billions
feed.summary: Intel is making a significant bet on advanced chip packaging, a process that combines multiple small chiplets onto a single custom chip, with the potential to generate billions in revenue. This strategy positions Intel against market leader TSMC and aims to capitalize on the increasing demand for custom AI chips. The company has invested heavily in facilities like its Rio Rancho plant and is reportedly in talks with major tech companies like Google and Amazon for its packaging services, with CEO Lip-Bu Tan and CFO Dave Zinsner highlighting its importance for future growth.

feed.title: SMCI Investors Have Opportunity to Join Super Micro Computer, Inc. Fraud Investigation with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm is investigating Super Micro Computer, Inc. (NASDAQ: SMCI) following a Reuters report detailing charges against three individuals, including the company's co-founder, for allegedly smuggling $2.5 billion worth of U.S. AI technology to China. This news led to a nearly 28.5% drop in Super Micro's share price. The firm is encouraging investors who suffered losses to participate in their fraud investigation.

feed.title: SMCI Investors Have Opportunity to Join Super Micro Computer, Inc. Fraud Investigation with the Schall Law Firm
